role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified the absence of linguistic cues (no signs) and stayed within domain.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Focused on terrain, soil, and vegetation patterns consistent with the role.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Analyzed plant types (xeric scrub) and biome compatibility.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Evaluated road infrastructure, poles, and markings.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized cross-domain cues (architecture, vehicle types, camera artifacts).

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The pipeline correctly identified the region (Sub-Saharan Africa) and the specific biome (Kalahari/Namib). The agents provided high-quality, image-grounded evidence. The final failure to distinguish between Namibia and Botswana is due to the extreme visual similarity of the landscape and infrastructure in this specific region, rather than agent error.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Descriptions of arid scrub, sandy soil, and flat terrain match the image perfectly.
visual_consistency_notes.botanics: Descriptions of xeric scrub and shrubs match the vegetation visible.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Descriptions of wooden poles and gravel shoulders match the infrastructure.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Descriptions of the white pickup and thatched roofs match the image.

tournament_failure.failure_reasoning: The image shows a generic arid road with wooden poles and thatched huts, features shared by both Botswana and Namibia. The agents and judge correctly identified the ambiguity ('nearly indistinguishable') but the judge had to pick a winner based on minor infrastructural cues (pole lean) that are not definitive. The visual evidence is genuinely under-determined.
